title: rhodosciadium montanum
intro: Rhodosciadium montanum, also known as the mountain carrot or mountain parsley, is a plant species that belongs to the Carrot family (Apiaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America, characterized by its fern-like leaves and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and antispasmodic effects. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and skin conditions.
